result:
Round 6 complete. Plain-text summary follows.

UNIT E (macros), round 6 — closed the "bare-nullary resolution fires too broadly" gap.

WHAT WAS WRONG
The `expand_term` `Var` arm resolved a nullary macro's bare name on the SORT decoration alone (`sort == Untagged`), ignoring the two other decorations the consumer's var node (`VarSpec`) carries: the numeric `idx` and the optional `typ`. Round 4 [Q34] had probed sorts (`~konst`/`$konst` stay variables) but never an INDEXED bare name. So `Var{name:"konst", idx:1}` — the AST form of `konst.1` — was wrongly resolved to the macro body.

ORACLE PROBES (logged QUERIES.log [Q43] rule-term, [Q44] formula; captures in macros/workspace/captures/bare_indexed*.out, bare_typed.out, bare_formula_*.out)
Full decoration matrix for `macros: konst() = h('k')`, identical in rule-term and formula positions:
- plain `konst` (untagged, idx 0, no type) → RESOLVES to `h('k')` (guarded formula confirms it in formula position).
- `konst.1` / `konst.2` / `konst.0` (untagged + any explicit index) → PARSE ERROR ("unexpected '.'"); an indexed nullary-macro name is never a macro use.
- `~konst.1` / `$konst.1` (sort + index) → stay ordinary variables (sort already blocks).
- `konst:msg` (type annotation) → PARSE ERROR (reconfirms [Q32]).
- NON-macro baselines `notmac.1` in both a rule term and a formula → parse fine as an ordinary indexed variable, proving the `.`-index is legal on a plain variable and the parse errors are specific to a nullary-MACRO name.
Conclusion: only the fully-UNDECORATED plain name is a bare-nullary use; index and type each block resolution on top of the sort rule. In the consumer's AST (macro resolution deferred to `expand`) a decorated `Var` must stay a variable — the sort-only guard fired too broadly.

FIX (macros/workspace/macro-clean/src/lib.rs, `expand_term` `Var` arm, ~line 158-168)
Resolve only when `formals.is_empty() && v.sort == SortHint::Untagged && v.idx == 0 && v.typ.is_none()`. The arm is shared by `expand` (full close) and `expand_staged`, so the tightened guard applies in BOTH modes with one edit. Note `konst.0` is indistinguishable from plain `konst` in the AST (both idx 0), so `idx==0` is the faithful resolving predicate; the reference rejects the surface `.0` at parse regardless. Module-doc summary updated to match.

TESTS (macros/workspace/macro-clean/src/tests.rs, new ROUND 6 block, added helpers `ivar`/`tvar`)
- `bare_nullary_indexed_not_resolved` — `konst.1`/`konst.2` stay variables while plain `konst` (idx 0) still resolves (contrast in the same rule).
- `bare_nullary_typed_not_resolved` — `konst:msg` stays a variable.
- `bare_nullary_indexed_and_sorted_not_resolved` — `~konst.1`/`$konst.1` stay variables.
- `bare_nullary_indexed_guard_in_formula_both_modes` — index guard in a lemma formula under BOTH `expand` and `expand_staged`; `konst.1` unresolved, plain `konst` resolved.
Verified load-bearing: temporarily removing `&& v.idx == 0 && v.typ.is_none()` fails 3 of the 4 new tests; restored guard passes.

ACCEPTANCE
`macros/workspace/run_tests.sh`: 30 cargo tests (26 prior + 4 new), 7 byteparity rule fixtures, 4 formula_parity fixtures — ALL PASS. No build warnings. Every prior test/fixture stays green (their bare uses are idx 0 / no type, so they still resolve).

EVIDENCE UPDATED
- macros/workspace/QUERIES.log — [Q43], [Q44] + round-6 fix note.
- macros/workspace/BEHAVIOR.md — §2.5 rewritten with the decoration-matrix table (sort/index/type), §4 traversal bullet tightened to "undecorated".
- macros/workspace/REPORT.md — appended "Round 6" section.
- New probes: macros/workspace/probes/bare_indexed.spthy, bare_indexed_zero.spthy, bare_indexed_sorted.spthy, bare_indexed_baseline.spthy, bare_typed.spthy, bare_indexed_formula.spthy, bare_formula_plain.spthy, bare_formula_indexed_baseline.spthy (captures alongside in macros/workspace/captures/).

Access discipline respected: no files under /home/kamilner/tamarin-rs/crates/, no *.hs sources, and no INTEGRATION_REPORT.md were read; every behavioral claim traces to a logged oracle probe.

Round-6 audit appended to `/home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/macros/AUDIT.md`.

Scope: this round's delta only (from clean-room HEAD 75807c0) under `macros/` — one guard-tightening in `expand_term`'s `Var` arm (`sort==Untagged` → `formals.is_empty() && sort==Untagged && idx==0 && typ.is_none()`) plus supporting BEHAVIOR/REPORT/QUERIES docs, 4 new tests (+2 helpers), and 8 new probe/capture pairs.

Findings: none (0). Abstraction-filtration-comparison outcome:
- Different mechanism, structurally. The reference resolves bare nullary macro names at PARSE (`nullaryApp`, Term.hs:143 emits an `fApp fs []` App by matching a bare token against `funSyms ∪ macroNames`; `addMacrosToSignature` injects arity-0 funsyms). It never holds a "decorated Var that might be a macro"; decoration-blocking is an emergent grammar property surfaced as parse errors. The clean unit has no signature/parser and resolves on a `Term::Var`'s own AST fields. The 4-way conjunction is dictated by the round-1 vendored AST (`VarSpec{name,idx,sort,typ}`, ast.rs:351) plus the boundary-observed fact that each decoration blocks resolution — merger.
- `idx==0` is the AST encoding of "no explicit index," not a smuggled constant; the `.0` rejection is boundary-observable (`bare_indexed_zero.out`).
- No upstream identifier/comment/constant leaks — grepped the diff for `applyMacros`/`macroToFunSym`/`nullaryApp`/`addMacrosToSignature`/`findMatchingMacro`/`macroNames`/`NoEq`/`Destructor`/`BNMacro`/... = zero hits. Quoted surface tokens (`konst`, `~konst`/`$konst`, `konst.1`, `konst:msg`) are all boundary-observable in probe inputs and captured parse-error output.

Provenance: every behavioral claim [Q43,Q44] traces to a logged probe with a matching committed capture; I read all 8 captures and they corroborate each row (parse errors for indexed/typed macro names; `notmac.1` baseline parses as an ordinary indexed var; plain `konst` resolves to `h('k')`). `#[test]` count in tests.rs = 30, matching REPORT's 26+4 claim. Round-4's previously capture-less `konst:msg`/[Q32] sub-claim now has a saved capture. No BEHAVIORAL redo instructions issued.

VERDICT: pass

-------------------------------------------------------------------
GAP 1 — summary content the R1–R4 model got wrong or missed
-------------------------------------------------------------------
Probed self-authored theories (not the GPL corpus), split-stream, on --prove and default.

1. Falsified verdict is KIND-DEPENDENT, not the bare `falsified` R2 guessed:
   - all-traces  → `falsified - found trace (N steps)`
   - exists-trace→ `falsified - no trace found (N steps)`   [r5_falsify_prove]
   The old render produced `falsified` — corrected.
2. Verified is uniform (`verified`, no suffix) for both kinds.  [r5_exists_verified]
3. Bounded/incomplete --prove runs add NO extra summary line — still
   `analysis incomplete (<explored> steps)`; only the step count grows
   (nonce_secrecy under --bound=2 → "6 steps").  [r5_nslpk3_prove_bound2]
4. Warning + lemmas in ONE theory (the previously-unobserved case):
   body layout is an opening `  ` (two-space) line, then the present sections
   joined by a `  ` line — warning section first, then lemma lines. Exact bytes:
       "  \n"
       "  WARNING: <N> wellformedness check failed!\n"          (noun stays singular)
       "           The analysis results might be wrong!\n"      (only under --prove; 11-sp
                                                                 indent = width of "  WARNING: ")
       "  \n"                                                    (separator, only if lemmas follow)
       "  <name> (<kind>): <verdict> (<N> steps)\n" ...
   The advisory 2nd line tracks the --prove FLAG, not proof success: a --prove run
   whose lemma prefix matches nothing still emits it [r5_falsify_nomatch]; a plain
   analyze run never does [r5_warn_lemma_default]; a warning-free run never shows it
   regardless of --prove [r5_nslpk3_prove_bound2].
5. No warnings + no lemmas → body is just the opening `  \n`.  [r5_nolemma_default]
6. Between-theory joining: a single blank `\n` before each `analyzed:` header,
   confirmed with lemmas-only + warn+lemma theories combined.  [r5_multi_warn_prove]

Model change: `Summary` now carries `warnings: Option<WarningSummary>` and
`lemmas: Vec<LemmaOutcome>` (replacing the flat `SummaryEntry` list). Falsified
text is computed from (result, kind); the advisory line is an explicit
`WarningSummary::analysis_maybe_wrong` flag the caller sets from --prove.

-------------------------------------------------------------------
GAP 2 — incremental (streaming) emission
-------------------------------------------------------------------
New `src/emit.rs`:
- `Sink::emit(stream, text)` — consumer's byte destination; consumer owns flush
  timing (flush-per-call = true line streaming; buffering = coalesced).
- `StreamCollector` — reference Sink accumulating into `Streams`.
- `BatchEmitter` — driven event-by-event: begin (preamble→stderr) / progress,
  closed_phases, extra_progress (→stderr) / payload (→stdout) / record_summary
  (buffered) / finish (combined summary→stdout). The summary is the one
  unavoidable trailing barrier — it spans every theory, matching the reference
  which prints it last.
- `drive_batch` — convenience driver over &[BatchTheory].
Equivalence: for identical inputs the emitter's PER-STREAM bytes equal
frame_batch's (cross-stream wall-clock interleaving differs — the point of
streaming — but each stream's byte sequence is identical). Proven by
`streaming_matches_assembled_on_shared_inputs` (5 shapes incl. empty,
extra-progress, warn+lemma, output-file-no-payload),
`streaming_reproduces_captured_multifile_streams` (vs real capture), and
`streaming_manual_drive_orders_streams_correctly` (hand-driven).

-------------------------------------------------------------------
10-LINE SUMMARY
-------------------------------------------------------------------
1. Falsified verdict is kind-dependent: `falsified - found trace` (all-traces) /
   `falsified - no trace found` (exists-trace) — not a bare `falsified`.
2. Verified is uniform (`verified`) for both trace kinds.
3. Bounded/incomplete --prove runs add no summary line — just `analysis incomplete (<explored> steps)`.
4. Warning+lemma summary: warning section first, `  ` separator, then lemma lines.
5. Under --prove a warning gains a 2nd 11-space-aligned line `The analysis results might be wrong!`; default omits it.
6. That advisory tracks the --prove flag, independent of whether any lemma was proved.
7. Body = opening `  ` line + sections (warning, lemmas) joined by `  ` lines; empty theories show only the opening line.
8. Multi-theory blocks are joined by one blank `\n` before each `analyzed:`.
9. framing.rs restructured to warnings/lemmas; emit.rs adds a Sink/BatchEmitter streaming API with the consumer owning flush timing.
10. Streaming path proven byte-equal per stream to assembled frame_batch; 45 tests pass, clippy clean, all prior fixtures untouched.

## ITEM 1 — origin-aware page shell (round-3 negative now closed)

The round-3 negative ([L15]) was about *network* origin. The **observable** distinction is the *theory's* load origin: **Local** (loaded from an on-disk file — command line, or a version derived from one) vs **Upload** (`POST /`, no on-disk file). Byte-comparing `overview/help` of a command-line Tutorial vs the same Tutorial re-uploaded (index-normalized full-page diff): **exactly two** north-bar `<li>` items differ, nothing else; HTTP headers identical modulo derived `Date`/`Content-Length`.

Full kind×origin matrix pinned ([R61]):

| | Reload file | Append modified lemmas |
|-|:-:|:-:|
| trace + Local | ✓ | ✓ |
| trace + Upload | – | – |
| equiv + Local | ✓ | – |
| equiv + Upload | – | – |

So **Reload** ⇔ `origin==Local`; **Append** ⇔ `origin==Local && kind==trace` (reconciles round-4 [R48] — the equiv shell's absent Append was the kind gate; its present Reload was the Local command-line load). Origin is **inherited** through proof ops ([R62]), so it is a per-version theory property (`Meta.origin`), not a web-layer choice. Threaded via `page::Origin{Local,Uploaded}` → `PageParams.origin`; `shell_template` gained a `§RELOAD§` slot (`RELOAD_ITEM`) beside `§APPEND§`; `Server::get_overview` passes `meta.origin`. Four live-captured fixtures `/home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/weblayer/workspace/web-clean/tests/fixtures/r6_overview_{trace,equiv}_{local,upload}.html` with byte-parity tests for all four cells + a dispatch-level thread-through/inheritance test. Fresh-server determinism cross-check [R63] confirmed reproducibility.

## ITEM 2 — state-delegation redesign (`StateOps`)

`Server` no longer owns the `BTreeMap`/counter. Every state operation is extracted to a new **`StateOps`** trait (separate from `ProverOps`, sharing `type Theory` — documented choice, since the interop story has the state backend as a distinct async component): `insert_new` (monotonic allocation), `get` (retrieval), `replace` (in-place mutation), `entries` (enumeration), `remove` (deletion). `Server<P: ProverOps, S: StateOps<Theory=P::Theory> = InMemoryState<P::Theory>>` keeps all dispatch/transport/envelope logic and drives state only through `S`. `InMemoryState<T>` is the in-memory reference impl; `Server::new` uses it (base at index 1), `Server::with_state` injects a custom backend. The probed lifecycle (monotonic allocation, counter monotonicity, retention of prior versions, in-place vs new-version) is the documented **contract** on the trait.

Honesty note: `remove` (deletion) is on the trait for backend-ownership completeness but is **not invoked by any current route** — under the observed retention invariant the web layer never drops a version; it is a contract-surface method. The trait is a synchronous facade; the consumer's async cache adapts behind it (the contract pinned is the lifecycle, which is what the observed behaviour constrains).

- `tests/parity.rs` (+4 origin tests), `tests/dispatch6.rs` (new, 7 tests), `tests/dispatch{,4,5}.rs` + `examples/corpus_html.rs` (`Meta.origin`/`PageParams.origin` propagation)
- Docs: `workspace/BEHAVIOR.md` §16, `workspace/QUERIES.log` [R60]–[R63], `workspace/REPORT.md` round-6 section

All prior tests remain byte-identical (they resolve to the default `InMemoryState` and `Origin::Local`).

UNIT B, round 7 — group wrap budget refined. Plain-text summary:

**Datum reproduced first.** Live `Wide` conclusion group `[Ack flat25, Big flat68, Out flat11]`: the reference packs **8** tuple elements on `Big`'s line 0 (effective budget ~56), one MORE than the flat-sum `87−(25+11)=51` yields (7). Reproduced byte-exact against the live capture (`wide_group.dot`).

**The exact budget function `f(flats)` — trigger and fill are DIFFERENT budgets:**
- **Wrap TRIGGER** (does a cell break): the flat-sum budget `budget_i = max(87 − Σ_{j≠i} flat_j, 20)`, wrap iff `flat_i > budget_i`. Re-verified exact via clean 2-/3-cell single-atom sweeps (floor-20 and single-cell-87 re-confirmed as special cases). **Order is definitively irrelevant** (batchG: `[A,B]≡[B,A]` and every 3-cell permutation identical, even at the margin with wrapping siblings).
- **FILL width** (how a broken cell packs): *wider* than the trigger budget, because a sibling that itself wraps occupies only the width it is ALLOCATED, not its flat. Computed by a **smallest-flat-first greedy allocation**: process cells by increasing flat; each cell's fill budget is `max(87 − Σ others' allocations, 20)` where a processed sibling contributes `min(flat, its budget)` and an un-processed (wider) one its full flat. For `Wide`: `Out`(11) fits; `Ack`, seen while `Big` is still flat 68, is squeezed to 20 (breaks after `~n.4`); `Big`, placed last, sees `Ack`'s allocation 20 → `87−20−11 = 56` → 8 elements. Continuation lines pack to the **same** effective budget as the first line (not the full width).

**Cross-validated against the corpus.** Fill byte-exactness over 142 540 wrapping prem/concl cells: flat-sum 41.45 % → sibling-occ 43.29 % → greedy `min(flat,budget)` **44.11 %** (the model implemented, and the only one that reproduces the Wide datum exactly).

**Residual, decomposed honestly** (the census/trigger stays 99.55 % cell / 98.3 % record — my change preserves the trigger, so this is unchanged; it is the *ceiling* for a rendering-crate closed form): of 2 779 prem/concl trigger mispredictions — 1 320 are occ-relief false-positives (a small cell fits beside a wide wrapping sibling; the greedy `fits` coupling, uncapturable by any closed rule — guarded occ-relief regresses to 0.82 %), and 1 459 false-negatives of which **954 (65 %) are decided on the UNABBREVIATED term** (e.g. `St_3_eNB(…KD19…EN2…)` displays at flat 48 but expands past 87), which is structurally outside a crate that receives post-abbreviation cell text. Remainder ~505 = ±1 HughesPJ `fits` boundary. All residuals trace to the GPL pretty-printer's `fits`/`fillSep` over the pre-abbreviation document.

**Implemented** in `graph-clean::generate::group_cells` (threaded through `wrap_cell_budget`; `render.rs` factored a shared `layout_cell`). Test `wide_conclusion_group_fill_byte_exact` added (fixture `wide_record.dot`). Corpus round-trip **12022/12022** byte-exact; whole suite green.

**Methodology note / trap recorded:** equal-length filler atoms across sibling facts collide (occ≥2, ≥10 chars) → get abbreviated → corrupt the measured flats; the first equal-sibling/3-cell sweeps showed phantom "+3" deviations that vanished once each fact used a distinct fill char.

Key files (all absolute, inside the cluster): `/home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/graphdot/workspace/graph-clean/src/generate.rs`, `/home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/graphdot/workspace/graph-clean/src/render.rs`, `/home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/graphdot/workspace/graph-clean/tests/generate_tests.rs`, `/home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/graphdot/workspace/graph-clean/tests/fixtures/wide_record.dot`; updated `/home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/graphdot/workspace/BEHAVIOR.md` (§3f, §8), `/home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/graphdot/workspace/QUERIES.log` (Session 7), `/home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/graphdot/workspace/REPORT2.md` (Round 7); probe rig under `/home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/graphdot/workspace/r7/`.

Delta audited (working tree vs clean HEAD 75807c0, graphdot/ only): `src/generate.rs` `group_cells` gains a two-budget FILL model, `src/render.rs` extracts `layout_cell`, one new byte-exact test + `wide_{group,record}.dot` fixtures, and Session-7 records in BEHAVIOR.md/QUERIES.log/REPORT2.md.

Both-sides finding: the sole new machinery (smallest-flat-first greedy fill-budget allocation) is structurally, numerically, and in exactness unlike the upstream `renderBalanced`/`scaleIndent` in Dot.hs. Upstream is a single proportional pass (`max(30, round(1.3·100·uᵢ/Σu))`, ×1.5 indent); the clean code is a subtractive/iterative two-budget model (trigger `max(87−Σothers,20)` + greedy `min(flat,budget)` fill). On the target datum `[Ack 25, Big 68, Out 11]`, upstream yields line-widths `[31,85,30]` vs clean `[20,51,20]`/`[20,56,20]` — no resemblance; and the clean model deliberately underfits (44.11% vs a transcription's ~100%), which is affirmative evidence of non-access. Source constants (100/1.3/130/30/1.5) absent; clean constants (87, 20) are probe-pinned boundaries logged in batchB/C. `layout_cell` is a DRY refactor on a `'#'`/`'['` string-shape predicate the typed-Doc source never uses.
